name: Garcia de Sola Reservoir and Herrera del Duque Castle Loop
name_original: Embalse de Garcia de Sola y Castillo de Herrera del Duque — circular desde Herrera del Duque
description: This difficult 37.7-mile touring cycling route offers challenging terrain and stunning views of the Embalse de Garcia de Sola and Herrera de
region_intro: Touring cycling around Herrera Del Duque features routes that navigate a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, natural water bodies, and varied terrain. The region includes features such as the Garcia de Sola Reservoir and the Peña Amarilla Gorge, offering diverse cycling experiences. Routes often traverse areas with hills and moderate to difficult climbs, providing challenges for experienced cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Herrera Del Duque The most popular touring cycling route is Garcia de Sola Reservoir and Herrera del Duque Castle Loop, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the reservoir and historical castle. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Peña Amarilla Gorge Loop Trail, a difficult 36.6 miles (58.9 km) path. This route explores the dramatic landscapes of the Peña Amarilla Gorge. Local touring cyclists also love the Playa hormigonada de Peloche loop from Dehesa Boyal, a 12.5 miles (20.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain near Peloche, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Herrera Del Duque is defined by reservoirs, gorges, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and advanced cycling trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Herrera Del Duque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Herrera Del Duque?
A: Touring cycling routes around Herrera Del Duque are primarily characterized by significant elevation changes, making many of them challenging. The region features 4 moderate routes and 3 difficult routes, offering options for intermediate to experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, there are several moderate routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa hormigonada de Peloche loop from Dehesa Boyal&lt;/a&gt; is a 12.5-mile (20.2 km) route that takes about 1 hour 35 minutes, offering varied terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flooded country road – Helechosa de los Montes loop from Helechosa de los Montes&lt;/a&gt;, which is also moderate.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by significant natural and historical landmarks. You can expect to see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garcia de Sola Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is a prominent feature in the region. Some routes also offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5667292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera del Duque Town Center&lt;/a&gt; and the dramatic landscapes of the Peña Amarilla Gorge.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Herrera Del Duque?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Herrera Del Duque are designed as loops. Notable examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021420835/garcia-de-sola-reservoir-and-herrera-del-duque-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garcia de Sola Reservoir and Herrera del Duque Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021425349/pena-amarilla-gorge-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Amarilla Gorge Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, both offering comprehensive tours of their respective areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Herrera Del Duque?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Herrera Del Duque is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Herrera Del Duque?
A: The terrain around Herrera Del Duque is varied, featuring significant elevation changes, natural water bodies like the Garcia de Sola Reservoir, and areas with hills. You will encounter both paved and unpaved sections, traversing diverse landscapes from gorges to reservoir shores.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Herrera Del Duque?
A: While many routes involve moderate to difficult climbs, some of the shorter, moderate loops might be suitable for families with older, more experienced children. It's advisable to check the specific route details and elevation profiles on komoot to ensure it matches your family's cycling ability.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Herrera Del Duque?
A: More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Herrera Del Duque's varied terrain. Users often praise the region for its diverse cycling experiences, including views of the reservoir and historical castle, and the dramatic landscapes of the Peña Amarilla Gorge.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021420835/garcia-de-sola-reservoir-and-herrera-del-duque-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garcia de Sola Reservoir and Herrera del Duque Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the reservoir and the historical castle. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2884436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Palacio de Cíjara and Reservoirs&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight that can be incorporated into or seen from nearby routes, providing expansive vistas.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Herrera Del Duque?
A: Public transport options directly to the start points of specific cycling routes in Herrera Del Duque may be limited. It is generally recommended to travel by car to reach the starting locations, especially for routes that begin outside the main town centers. Check local bus schedules for connections to Herrera Del Duque itself.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Herrera Del Duque?
A: Parking is typically available in or near the towns and villages that serve as starting points for the routes, such as Herrera del Duque, Peloche, Helechosa de los Montes, or Fuenlabrada de los Montes. Look for public parking areas in these settlements before embarking on your ride.

Q: Are there any advanced cycling trails for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Experienced touring cyclists will find challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021420835/garcia-de-sola-reservoir-and-herrera-del-duque-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garcia de Sola Reservoir and Herrera del Duque Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 37.7-mile (60.7 km) trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021425349/pena-amarilla-gorge-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Amarilla Gorge Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which explores dramatic landscapes over 36.6 miles (58.9 km).
tips: Road that runs along the banks of the Garcia de Sola reservoir toward the town of Peloche. The road winds along, offering incredible views of the reservoir and its surroundings.

The García de Sola Reservoir is an impressive work of engineering located in the Extremadura region, near Talarrubias, in the province of Badajoz. The reservoir is located in an environment of great beauty, surrounded by imposing rock formations and dense vegetation composed of Mediterranean forests, pine forests and eucalyptus trees. It is a place of great value for birdlife, with species such as the griffon vulture, black vulture, black stork, golden eagle and peregrine falcon.

The Herrera del Duque Castle, located in the province of Badajoz (Extremadura), is an impressive 15th-century fortress that dominates the landscape of the La Siberia region from the top of the Sierra Chamorro.
It is said that wailing sounds can be heard at night from the "Tower of Sighs," and there are legends about a hidden treasure protected by a curse.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring bicycle route is approximately 60.7 kilometers (37.7 miles) long. It typically takes around 4 hours and 30 minutes to complete, but this can vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is demanding, classified as a difficult touring bicycle ride requiring very good fitness. While a significant portion may be paved, expect some unpaved and difficult segments where you might need to push your bike. The route features 'savage' climbs, including tough single-track sections with rocks and loose surfaces.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and requires 'very good fitness'. The challenging terrain, including unpaved sections and steep climbs, makes it unsuitable for beginners or those with limited cycling experience.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular views, especially as you ascend to the Collado de la Consolación. Key highlights include the beautiful stretch next to the Garcia de Sola reservoir, the Garcia de Sola Reservoir itself with its unique granite rock formations, and the imposing Herrera del Duque Castle. The route is set within La Siberia Biosphere Reserve, offering diverse landscapes of Mediterranean forest, pine woods, and eucalyptus trees.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse de Garcia de Sola and Castillo de Herrera del Duque circular route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Herrera del Duque, you can typically find parking within the town. Look for public parking areas in Herrera del Duque to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this cycling route are not detailed, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas like the Biosphere Reserve, to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety. Be mindful of the challenging terrain if cycling with a dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To avoid the intense summer heat of Extremadura, the best times to cycle this route are during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). These seasons offer more pleasant temperatures and often beautiful natural scenery.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It is publicly accessible.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino Natural del Guadiana, Camino del Levante - Etapa 3, and Camino peatonal Herrera - Peloche.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter along the Embalse de Garcia de Sola?
faqs.answer: The Embalse de Garcia de Sola is known for vultures nesting in its rocky formations. As the route is within a Biosphere Reserve, you may also encounter other diverse wildlife typical of the Iberian Peninsula's well-preserved ecosystems.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's essential to bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle, and appropriate cycling gear. Due to unpaved sections, a mountain bike or a touring bike with robust tires is recommended. Always check the weather forecast and dress accordingly.

waypoints.name: Beautiful stretch next to the Garcia de Sola reservoir.
